entry.title: Re: Archiving question
entry.content: Check this  blog from @meow

entry.title: Re: check if object is locked by user ?
entry.content: HI Thank you very much for wanting to help and donating your time The option you suggested is not available to me I can't register salesOrder.IsLockedByUser() I am attaching the code with which I thought to perform the test Maybe this will give you a direction on how I can check if an adder is locked The problem is in the code that when it runs in a loop and checks whether an order is locked or not After he found some locked order It does not continue to recheck properly but as if treating all other orders as if they were locked For example if order number 150 is currently locked So all orders after this number are also considered locked below is my code : ------------------------------------------------ var currentDate = Context.GetCurrentGlobalDateTime(); var currentDate1 = currentDate.ConvertToLocalNormalisedDateTime(); var SalesOrders = SalesOrder.QueryByElements; var SalesOrderDate_SelParams = SalesOrders.CreateSelectionParams(); SalesOrderDate_SelParams.Add(SalesOrders.Status.ItemListCustomerOrderLifeCycleStatusCode, "I", "EQ", "1"); SalesOrderDate_SelParams.Add(SalesOrders.Status.ItemListCustomerOrderLifeCycleStatusCode, "I", "EQ", "2"); var SalesOrderData_Results = SalesOrders.Execute(SalesOrderDate_SelParams); var SalesOrderData_ResultsCopy = SalesOrders.Execute(SalesOrderDate_SelParams); foreach (var SO in SalesOrderData_ResultsCopy) { var uuid; uuid = Library::UUID.ParseFromString(SO.UUID.content.ToString());// sales order id var messages; var lock; lock = BOAction.CheckLock("SalesOrder", "http://sap.com/xi/AP/CRM/Global", "Root", uuid); messages = lock.MessageTypeItem; var validationFailed : Common:Indicator=false; var a; if (!lock.IsInitial() && messages.Count() > 0) { foreach (var message in messages) { if (message.MessageSeverityText == "E" && message.MessageID.content == "CL_CDA_DOCHD/000") { validationFailed = true; a = SalesOrderData_Results.Remove(ins => ins.ID.content == SO.ID.content); SalesOrderData_Results = a; lock.Clear(); } } messages.Clear(); } } ------------------------------------------------ I mean, actually my code is not completely correct and I probably don't check it in the right way Anyone who can help me, I would be very happy thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Just Ask Feature
entry.content:
Hi @himanshu_ts_sharma

Could you please let me know the difference between Just ask and Search to insight. Because I can see both the feature in the tenant.

Thanks in Advance.

#SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Internal table to binary for EXCEL
entry.content:
<html>

TRY.
        cl_salv_table=>factory(                               "#EC CI_NOORDER
          IMPORTING
            r_salv_table = DATA(r_salv)                          " Basis Class Simple ALV Tables
          CHANGING
            t_table      = t_intab
        ).
      CATCH cx_salv_msg. " ALV: General Error Class with Message
    ENDTRY.
    TRY.
        DATA(it_fcat) = cl_salv_controller_metadata=>get_lvc_fieldcatalog(
          EXPORTING
            r_columns      = r_salv->get_columns( )                 " ALV Filter
            r_aggregations = r_salv->get_aggregations( ) ).
      CATCH cx_salv_msg.
        RETURN.
    ENDTRY.
    READ TABLE it_fcat ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_fcat>) WITH KEY fieldname = 'PO_LIFNR'.
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      <fs_fcat>-reptext = 'PO Supplier'(003).
    ENDIF.
    TRY.
        cl_salv_bs_lex=>export_from_result_data_table(
          EXPORTING
            is_format            = if_salv_bs_lex_format=>mc_format_xlsx
            ir_result_data_table =  cl_salv_ex_util=>factory_result_data_table(
          EXPORTING
            r_data              =  r_data
            t_fieldcatalog      = it_fcat )
          IMPORTING
            er_result_file       = DATA(er_result_file) ).
      CATCH cx_salv_unexpected_param_value. " Unexpected value in method parameter
    ENDTRY.
*  t_btab = cl_bcs_convert=>xstring_to_solix( er_result_file ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
      EXPORTING
        buffer        = er_result_file
      IMPORTING
        output_length = len
      TABLES
        binary_tab    = t_btab.

</html>

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA XS Advanced Application - Node.js service Frequently crashed
entry.content:
Dear Gregorw,

As per your suggestion, We are change the node version "node": "12.x". Still we are facing same issue.

9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   #
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   # Fatal error in , line 0
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   # Check failed: IsEmpty().
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   #
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   #
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   #
9/10/24 2:50:06.642 PM [APP/53-0] ERR   #FailureMessage Object: 0x7fff94e9e070

entry.title: Re: Datasphere performance issue
entry.content:
Hi, 

# SAC crashes - OoM?

# In DSP you can get PlanViz file, which you can analyze in Studio. Could help, but not for the SAC Crash.

# For SAC there is the the Performance Analysis Tool. But before you deep dive there, go to DSP. The 40 seconds runtime is quite a lot for the 20M entries. First you need to get the runtime in DSP down - can bypass by persisting the view and consuming in SAC via analytic model.

I would: 

1. lower the complexity. Remove the calculations in step#6 / remove the step #6 . Check the runtime. I assume the calculations between step #2 and #3 are necessary for the union in stepp #3, if not, remove them as well.  Check if you go for runtime < 10 seconds. 

2. persist this view. In the same task chain, where you have the COPA source tables, add step to persist this view and then re-use it in analytic model, which you consume in SAC. 

3. How do you connect DSP to SAC? What do you consume? Try analytic model -  Worked for me the best way. 

Martin

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - Remote Table dynamic filter pushdown
entry.content:
Hi @RG20 ,

sadly i am not sure. Maybe you can test some variances like different data types or expressions to push down.
BR,

Thore

entry.title: Re: Replication Flow : Tables not getting imported using...
entry.content: Please check SU53 logs in the source system for the RFC user that is used to connect from ECC to Datasphere for any authorization issue. You can also apply ST05 or ST01 trace for that RFC user and perform operation if nothing is seen in SU53.

could you please let me know if this is possible to disable the view of Talent Profile (Talent Flags, Successors, Nomination Portlet) from People Profile of Contingent Workers? 

Thank you in advance!

Regards,

Aneta

b) Standard network activity

c) Operative WBS element

entry.title: Re: Datasphere performance issue

entry.title: Re: Barcode Scanning function using ABAP

entry.title: Re: Enhanced RISE with SAP Methodology with clean core quality checks
entry.content:
Hi, for  Conduct Clean Core Quality Gate 4

the template is still missing. Any update on that? 

BR,

Mark

entry.title: Best practices for property exchange using ProcessDirect Adapter in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello SAP Cloud Integration experts,

I’m currently working on decomposing a large integration flow into smaller, modular flows that communicate via the ProcessDirect adapter. My goal is to make the consumer integration flows as dynamic as possible for reusability purposes. Given that the ProcessDirect adapter doesn’t support property propagation, I’m looking for best practices to pass properties between iFlows.

One option I’m considering is passing the properties as message headers, though I doubt that using headers for this is ideal. Another option is temporarily storing the properties in a datastore and using a correlation ID to retrieve them in the target flow, but would require some additional steps.

Is there a simpler or more efficient approach? Any recommendations or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: How to install DTW on client/workstation PC's with SAP Business One 10.0
entry.content:
We would like to install DTW on our client/workstation PCs so that our users can upload required information to system with DTW by themselves. Would anyone guide how to install DTW in client PC? Can user connect DTW with their individual credentials instead of 'MANAGER' credential?

We are using "SAP Business One 10.0 version for SAP HANA (10.00.201) FP 2208"

entry.title: HCM integrates with which other functional modules like MM,PP,SD,ETC...?And in which senerios?
entry.content: HCM is integrate with officially FICO but what about others! Try to explain with example.

entry.title: Re: SAP BPA Process Started but automation not triggered
entry.content: So I need a very expensive agent to run some REST calls because the normal actions do not allow to use expressions for input fields? Thats horrible. I just wanted to use 4 REST calls with the input of each other but because it is OData I need to change the filter to something with a static string I define and the variable I get from the previous call and it's not possible with actions. I can only do this in an automation. And for that I need an agent then that is, as I said, very expensive?

entry.title: Re: CAP CDS annotation for direct date picker
entry.content:
Hello @amallari,

In CAP, there is no annotation for Date fields like we do in ABAP CDS.

Please use annotation Capabilities on filter to display the DatePicker:

annotate YOUR_ENTITY with @Capabilities : {
    FilterRestrictions : {
        $Type              : 'Capabilities.FilterRestrictionsType',
        RequiredProperties : [ YOUR_PROPERTY ],
        FilterExpressionRestrictions : [
            {
                $Type : 'Capabilities.FilterExpressionRestrictionType',
                Property : YOUR_PROPERTY,
                AllowedExpressions : 'SingleRange' (please see documentation for other values)
            }
        ]
    }
};

Regards,
Sébastien

entry.title: Docusig CLM integration Ariba Contracts
entry.content:
I'm trying to set up the integration between Ariba Contracts and Docusign CLM but in Ariba I need to add some specific information; Specifies the URL context that is being integrated with SAP Ariba Contracts and Specifies the hostname that is being integrated with SAP Ariba Contracts.  I've looking informtacion about wich information the system needs but no luck so far, Does any body have an idea wich information is needed?

 

Thank you

entry.title: Einvoicing for India
entry.content:
Hello,

We are implementing Electronic Customer Invoices for India. Can someone please explain what is the exact difference between the Einvoice for Customers Legacy (SAP Cloud Integration or SAP Integration Suite ) and the new Einvoicing for customers (SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition). We need to understand if there is any difference besides the way to push to Tax authority/Government

Please find the Sap help portal links below;

Einvoice for Customers Legacy

Electronic Invoices (Legacy) | SAP Help Portal

Electronic Customer Invoice

Electronic Customer Invoices | SAP Help Portal

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Market Segments Apps showing unusual fiscal periods
entry.content: Hello @Susanne_Helbig, thank you for your input. Since these fiscal year variants are not used by the customer but shown i can however recommend to ignore them. TZhank oyu for your answer.

entry.title: Re: Best practices for property exchange using ProcessDirect Adapter in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content: For propagation of properties the headers would be your go to. The option of datastore is a LOT of overhead for managing exchange of properties. When appropriate use properties and when necessary go with headers... that would be my advice.

entry.title: Re: MDG BP search result list enhancement

https://www.sap.com/documents/2016/06/1009ddb7-757c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html

entry.title: Re: Internal table to binary for EXCEL
entry.content: What is your issue, what is your question?

entry.title: Re: What SPs in BO 4.3 still support UNV?
entry.content: Per the Statement of Direction and this blog, unv universes will not be deprecated until BusinessObjects 2025, so it's safe to say that they will be supported through the end of BI4.3.

entry.title: Re: Sap Business one SSO Not working when switching Database
entry.content:
Hello Andy,

We are facing similar issue have you found any solution for this?

entry.title: Re: Issue with concatenate. No blanks at the end. RESPECTING BLANKS not working fully.
entry.content:
Dear @Sandra_Rossi,
In my actual program I am not using GUI_UPLOAD, the code I send was my testing code, but the problem is the same. This is the code from my real program for saving file: 

IF lt_tab[] IS NOT INITIAL.
    OPEN DATASET lv_filename FOR OUTPUT IN TEXT MODE ENCODING DEFAULT.
    LOOP AT lt_tab INTO ls_tab.
      TRANSFER ls_tab TO lv_filename.
    ENDLOOP.
    CLOSE DATASET lv_filename.
    REFRESH lt_tab.
  ENDIF.

However, I did set trunc_trailing_blanks to false in GUI_DOWNLOAD. The result is the same. 
Do you have any other ideas how could I solve this?
And thank you so much for answering.

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA XS Advanced Application - Node.js service Frequently crashed
entry.content: Please check https://nodejs.org/en/about/previous-releases for what is the supported version. You would need to update the buildpack on your HANA XSA of couse also.

entry.title: Re: Barcode Scanning function using ABAP
entry.content: If you want to stay with SAP Standard you create a custom UI5 Application using the sap.ndc.BarcodeScannerButton control. The ABAP backend needs to provide the OData API that then processes the scaned code.

entry.title: Re: QC result recording after UD in SAP
entry.content:
Hi,

Did you select the option "with long-term inspection" on the selection screen?

entry.title: Re: Free-Hand SQL Rights missing in 4.2 SAP BI
entry.content: I've seen similar behavior after an upgrade - missing rights in the Applications tab in CMC.  I can't find the SAP Note right now, but from what I remember there were two solutions given.  The first is just to run a "Repair" installation.  If that doesn't work, then there's a process to manually copy .dfo files from one place to another.  For us, the second solution did the trick.

entry.title: Re: Seeking Guidance on Choosing the Right Learning Path for SAP Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Hi @HussainSiddiq ,

Thanks for writing. The first step is always to scan job openings to understand what is in demand for your local market.

With that said, SAP Finance skills are usually a safe bet as they combine two rather complex skill sets. Please read more here and tag Stefanos as he is our resident finance expert.

Best of luck!

entry.title: Re: Hello!
entry.content: Welcome aboard @moh_hafez1 !

entry.title: Re: Create Dynamic Table for SQL String
entry.content:
The code I have shared is if you can derive the fields/structure of the dynamic table during runtime. In the blog link that you have shared, the declaration of lt_comp is there.  But for the code snippet I have shared, I used a structure from the ABAP type  group - DATA lt_comp TYPE abap_component_tab therefore field symbol type is abap_component_tab as well, which has fields NAME and TYPE to store the fieldname and its corresponding data type of the dynamic table that will be built.  

I hope this is clear enough.

entry.title: Issue with Purchase Order Document Type Defaulting
entry.content:
Hi 

We've recently created new document types: ZQYS for purchase requisitions and ZQYS for purchase orders.

Problem: When creating a purchase order (PO) referencing a purchase requisition (PR) with document type ZQYS, the system is defaulting to the document type NB. We have to manually change the PO document type to ZQYS. Request: How can we configure the system to automatically set the purchase order document type to ZQYS when the referenced purchase requisition is also ZQYS? 

Thank you for your assistance.  


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Purchase Order Document Type Defaulting
entry.content:
Hello,

You can refer to the below help link:

Determining Document Types for Purchase Orders Created from Purchase Requisition Items

Thank you

SAP SUPPORT

entry.title: Re: Create Dynamic Table for SQL String
entry.content:
If you can determine the field name or reference during runtime:

For data element, lv_fieldstr = 'MATNR'.

For table field reference, lv_fieldstr = 'MARA-MATNR'.

entry.title: Re: Move GOS items from SOFFCONT1 to Content Server using RSIRPIRL with parameter "delete later
entry.content:
Please check note: 2991944 - Introducing the Delay mode in report RSIRPIRL

 

Symptom

You would like to execute report RSIRPIRL in delay Mode. In this new delay mode, your content deletion from the source repository will not be deleted immediately but it can happen at later point.
Also, if the documents are stored with file size as empty you would like to eliminate migration of these documents.

Reason and Prerequisites

We have found that sometimes there are issues with creating the documents in the target repository. If the documents are deleted from the source the retrieval becomes difficult.  This feature will help you with such issues.
If you have saved the documents without file size in File tables, after migrating those documents to the HTTP content server, the documents contents will not be saved on HTTP content server. This will result in data loss.

Solution

This Note delivers a new feature.

  • P_UPDATE - Enable the checkbox to avoid deletion of the documents from source repository immediately.
    System will mark the documents for deletion. Later when executing Report RSIR_CONTENT_UNMARK_PRELIM after 30 days, the documents from the actual source repository get deleted.
    • P_EMPTY - Enable the checkbox to avoid migration of documents between repositories with file size in File tables as '0'.

      Please Note: Please avoid executing the report with option 'Delete Later' (P_UPDATE = 'X') multiple times using the same date range as it will create inconsistencies in the system and may lead to data loss.

entry.title: Re: Usage of filter on multilevel expand
entry.content:
Assuming 2) returns all headers you want, and you only want to have items with material '123', you can extend the query with a nested filter on items:

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zsales_order_srv/srvd_a2x/sap/zsales_order/0001/SalesOrderHeader?$expand=_Item($filter=Material eq '123';$expand=_Schedule)&$filter=(_Item/any(s: s/Material eq '123')

entry.title: Re: Custom fields are not appearing in Sales reports
entry.content: For Sales Volume - Flexible Analysis - what is the actual Queries/View used? I don't see anything clearly how to enable a custom field for this app.

entry.title: Domain Assignment Through Connectors
entry.content:
Hi,

We would like to assign users to a domain by updating the Connectors section in:

Learning Admin > System Admin > Configuration > CONNECTORS

We tried adding users to one of two existing domains based on their organization ID using the code:

sfuser.field.transform.data.DMN_ID.DOMAIN1\ Population=Org1 (05)

sfuser.field.transform.data.DMN_ID.DOMAIN2\ Population=Org2 (80), Org3 (1), Glanbia Org4 (90)

Result:

The system created 2 new domains, which were copies of our 2 existing domains with the word 'population' appended

Domain 1 Population

Domain 2 Population

Question

Is there a way to assign users to the existing domains rather than have the system create new ones.

 

Thank you

Kind regards,

Susan

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content: Thanks for the introductions everyone and sorry for the delay!

entry.title: Re: B1iWS Connector - B1iServerURL does not update with n...
entry.content:
Hi Nidhi

Thanks for your reply.

I can confirm that the server name displays correctly in Cfg Runtime.

I conducted a reboot of the SAP server out of hours and it resolved the issue with the EDS then referencing the right server name for the connection.

Many thanks

entry.title: Self-introduction
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano

I’m Trang Nguyen, I have a diverse background in supply chain management, software development, and technology consulting. My professional journey has led me to explore the transformative capabilities of SAP, and I’m eager to deepen my expertise in this dynamic field.

With several years of experience in supply chain roles and a solid foundation in IT business analysis, I’ve developed a strong interest in how SAP can drive digital transformation and operational efficiency. My goal is to leverage SAP’s powerful tools to enhance business processes and contribute to innovative solutions within the digital landscape.

Through this thread, I look forward to engaging with fellow professionals, sharing insights, and exploring SAP’s digital initiatives. I’m excited to be part of this community and to learn from the wealth of knowledge here.

Best regards,
Trang Nguyen

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Conversion – Merge Customer and Vendor using Transaction CVI_LEDH (
entry.content:
Hi Andi, 

Are there any option to mass maintain report CVI_LEDH. Customer has a large volume of Cust/Vend that he once merged into one BP, and maintaining CVI_LEDH for more them 30 K entries is very time consuming ! 

Thank you ! 

Radu

entry.title: Introducing myself to the community
entry.content:
Hello, my name is Sarah.

I'm Brazilian and I've been working with SAP HCM for about 3 years. I went through a career change process and migrated to the IT area, with a degree in psychology.

I am studying and striving to pursue a career as an SAP consultant, and would like the opportunity to become certified and increase my knowledge and expertise in the area. Happy to join the community, thank you!

entry.content: Hello, could you fix it or find a workaround? Facing the same issue. Kr

entry.title: Re: Replicating table data from an SAP ECC system with SAP Datasphere using Replication Flows
entry.content: In a scenario in which I have an SLT that replicates in delta by ODQ from an ECC and then DS extracts the delta from SLT and writes the varied data into BigQuery Table. If I wanted to migrate to datasphere using those same delta queues so I don't have to repeat init, would that be possible?  Thank you

entry.title: Re: Multi-tier Collaborative Outsourcing Manufacturing Process with SAP Business Network
entry.content:
Hi @kalandi-nayak , 

As you mentioned that this process supports for both real-time and backflush consumption scenarios, are there any pre-requisites for real-time consumption updates? Can this be used for normal subcontracting purchase orders with item cat = L or is real time only limited to the purchase orders with acc assignment = F i.e created from production orders. and how would the real-time inventory be updated in SAP system? is it with the same 543 movement or other movement types?

entry.title: rule to delete attachment
entry.content: It is possible to delete attachment after 1 month?

entry.title: Final Call – Start Building Your Agenda :)
entry.content:
The agenda is finalized, the talk tracks are nailed down, and the coffee machines are buzzing in anticipation! This year’s SAP ALM Summit EMEA 2024 in Mannheim, Germany (Sep 24-27) is now less than two weeks away, and excitement is building among presenters and registered attendees. If you haven’t yet registered, this is your last chance to join our annual event. If you’ve already signed up, awesome, now it’s time to build your agenda! Head over to the web app to call up the schedule, view the floor plans, speaker and event details, and build your own order of the day.

The event app contains full details of the 4-day show, as well as your personal agenda builder

 

ALM-athon – Think “Eurovision Song Contest” (But with Fewer Costume Changes?)

One major summit highlight that’s definitely not to be missed is the innovative, inspirational — and in the meantime almost infamous — ALM-athon. Now in its fourth year, the ALM-athon is a fantastic and fun hackathon event aimed at SAP Partners who either leverage ALM solutions or develop their own new extensions on SAP Cloud ALM.

The kick-off and early rounds have already taken place, and the 6 chosen finalists will once again take center stage during the ALM Summit. With just 10 minutes to showcase their creations and to impress both a jury of experts AND the voting public, expect lots of style and pizzazz as each finalist tries to win votes and secure this year’s title, just like NTT Data did last October. Many of our past entrants have gone on to become commercialized and made available in the SAP Store, so there’s a lot to play for. Be sure to catch this unmissable part of the show!

ALM-athon: An annual highlight at the ALM Summit. Who will succeed NTT Data as this year's winner?

 

The venue is awesome, the people are amazing, and the topics this year are as fab as they’ve ever been, so don’t miss this last chance to register, sign up today to join us and other experts at the SAP ALM Summit EMEA 2024 in Mannheim.

Feel free to share this within your community network and get involved. And thanks as always for reading, caring, and getting involved! See you in two weeks!

entry.title: Re: how to stop migration cockpit long time running job
entry.content:
Hi Lily,

which steps do you do to "clear or delete the blocked activity"? In our case, a deletion task is stuck and running for >13 days, so there's no SM37 and no SM50 entry. Any recommendations?

 

Regards,

Andreas

entry.title: Re: Capacity Document Creation Settings in SAP TM (2022 Release)

entry.title: How can I Improve performance of Below code.
entry.content:
Hi, 

As per my requirement i fetched multiple Queries, but while preparing final Internal table. 
Taking lot of time. One Scenario having 10 Days having 19k Records. Any possible way I Can improve the performance.

* SORT lt_cdpos BY changenr.
* SORT lt_user_addr BY bname.
* SORT lt_usr21 BY bname.
* SORT lt_adr6 BY persnumber.
LOOP AT lt_cdhdr ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_cdhdr>).
READ TABLE lt_cdpos WITH KEY changenr = <ls_cdhdr>-changenr ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_cdpos>).
IF sy-subrc = 0.
READ TABLE lt_user_addr WITH KEY bname = <ls_cdhdr>-username ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_user_addr>).
IF sy-subrc = 0.
READ TABLE lt_usr21 WITH KEY bname = <ls_user_addr>-bname ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_usr21>).
IF sy-subrc = 0.
READ TABLE lt_adr6 WITH KEY persnumber = <ls_usr21>-persnumber ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_adr6>).
ENDIF.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.
APPEND VALUE #( uname = <ls_cdhdr>-username
udate = <ls_cdhdr>-udate
utime = <ls_cdhdr>-utime
tcode = <ls_cdhdr>-tcode
Objvale = <ls_cdhdr>-objectid
tabname = <ls_cdpos>-tabname
fname = <ls_cdpos>-fname
valueold = <ls_cdpos>-value_old
valuenew = <ls_cdpos>-value_new
fstname = <ls_user_addr>-name_first
lstname = <ls_user_addr>-name_last
email = <ls_adr6>-smtp_addr ) TO rt_output.
ENDLOOP.

entry.title: IBM MQ QA server not reachable from Cloud Connector
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We followed the same steps on both IBM MQ DEV & QA servers to establish the technical connection from Cloud Connector(hosted on our Organization data center). Our IBM MQ DEV server is reachable from Cloud connector whereas IBM MQ QA/INT server is not reachable. 

We did not import any certificate to  IBM MQ DEV server and it is reachable from Cloud Connector without any issues. Now, we are checking on why MQ QA is not reachable from Cloud Connector even after following same steps that is done for MQ DEV.. Please find the below screenshot regarding the error on Cloud connector while trying to reach MQ QA.

Thanks,

Ramu.

entry.title: Supply Chain in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
This blog post provides an overview of innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition release 2408 for the line of business Supply Chain. 

Table of Contents

Order Promising

  • Dynamic Third-Party Order Processing with Advanced ATP (‌‌‌‌▶️demo video)
  • CDS View Schedule Line With Open Requirement Quantity in Sales and Distribution

Inventory Management

  • Manage Physical Inventory Count app: Download–Upload Option (‌‌▶️demo video)
  • Manage Tolerances for Physical Inventory Differences (‌‌▶️demo video)
  • Special Stock E & K,  restricted-use stocks in Transfer Stock Apps (‌‌▶️demo video) 

Warehousing Management

  • Warehouse-Driven Split of the Outbound Delivery Order (▶️demo video)
  • Generation of Packaging Items (▶️demo video)
  • Partial Goods Receipt Posting (▶️demo video)

Transportation Management

  • Supplier Return with Transportation Management  (▶️demo video) + ...

More Information / Additional links

First watch the video from my teammate Markus Oertelt in which he presents his release highlights for supply chain management. 

Video 1: Watch the highlights by Markus Oertelt

 

Order Promising

Dynamic Third-Party Order Processing with Advanced ATP

Third-Party Order Processing (TPOP) with advanced ATP (aATP) now enables the processing of order items that are shipped to a subsidiary or plant. You can also use a new Business Add-In (BAdI) to modify the substitution result from Alternative-Based Confirmation (ABC) to, for example, exclude substitutions from being used for confirmations and influence their priority.

In addition to the existing shipment type Third-Party Direct Shipment, you can now define a sourcing profile for third-party substitution strategies with the new shipment type Bought-In Item. This means the third-party order items shall be shipped to an own plant or subsidiary (instead of to the customer directly). In the sales order, the corresponding subitems get item category CBAB and also lead to a full confirmation in the availability check.

Using the new BAdI, Enriched Substitution Result in aATP, you can adapt the substitution result found by ABC. You can remove found substitutes from the list (for example, because their source of supply shall not be used for confirmations) and influence the priority of substitutions used for confirmations.

For the above, the Configure Substitution Strategy app has been enhanced with Advanced Configuration options (Sourcing Profile, Follow-Up Strategies, and Business Add-In) at substitution strategy level. Furthermore, a new subscreen (Follow-Up Strategies) for the configuration of follow-up strategies has been added. You can use follow-up strategies to enable additional confirmations to be used to fulfill outstanding requested quantity for requirements. After the availability of a requirement has been checked, the follow-up strategy is applied to any remaining quantity that could not be confirmed by the substitution strategy (or the preceding follow-up strategy if multiple follow-up strategies have been assigned to the substitution strategy.) If multiple follow-up strategies are defined, they are applied in the sequence defined by the ordinal number in the Configure Substitution Strategy app.

Discover the intricacies of third-party order processing through three detailed cases that Andreas Krause showcase in the video below: delegation to supplier for fulfillment, dynamic fulfillment decision (starting at 2:21 min), and a scenario in which we do not want that our supplier send the goods directly to our customer (starting at 6:00 min).

Video 2: Dynamic Third-Party Order Processing with Advanced ATP

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

CDS View Schedule Line With Open Requirement Quantity in Sales and Distribution

The latest 2408 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition brings an exciting innovation in the area of extensibility. We released the new CDS View Schedule Line With Open Requirement Quantity in Sales and Distribution (CDS View Name I_SDScheduleLineWthOpenRqmtQty) which allows you to read for single open requirements in sales and distribution. From a technical standpoint, the data is read from table VBBE, which contains these entries.
This new CDS view opens up a world of possibilities for businesses looking to integrate their planning solutions with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. By consuming the data provided by the CDS view, companies can now seamlessly integrate their planning processes with their SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment.

Figure 1: CDS view 'Schedule Line With Open Requirement Quantity in Sales and Distribution'

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

 

Inventory Management

Manage Physical Inventory Count app: Download–Upload Option

The Manage Physical Inventory Count app has been enhanced. A new feature to download and upload Excel files for stock counting allow customers with 3rd-party warehouses to provide printouts to their staff. Once this kind of paper-based physical inventory process is completed, the results can be uploaded back into the system as an Excel file. Watch the next video in which I show you the new Excel download/upload option in Manage Physical Inventory Count app.

Video 3: Manage Physical Inventory Count app - download and upload Excel files

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

Manage Tolerances for Physical Inventory Differences 

Now you can maintain tolerance groups for the posting of physical inventory differences to define the maximum value difference a user is allowed to post in physical inventory. For this configuration step, you define tolerance groups and assign user to the tolerance groups.

Figure 2: Configuration Step 'Manage Tolerances for Physical Inventory Differences''

Watch the video below in which I show you the configuration step and its impact on the physical inventory count.

Video 4: Manage Tolerances for Physical Inventory Differences

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

Special Stock E & K,  restricted-use stocks in SAP FIORi apps for performing stock changes 

You can use different stock types and special stock types to perform stock changes. The following table gives you an overview about the stock types and special stock types that are supported by the SAP Fiori apps Manage Stock, Transfer Stock - In-Plant, and Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant. Blue highlighted stock types are newly supported since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. In detail: 

  • The special stock types Order Stock (E) and Consignment Stock (K) have been added to the Manage Stock and the Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant apps.
  • You can now also display the stock type Restricted-Use Stock in the Transfer Stock - In-Plant and Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant apps.

Figure 3: Supported Stock Types and Special Stock Types in SAP Fiori apps for Goods Movement

In the next video I show you the use of the new stock types and special stock types in the SAP Fiori apps for performing stock changes.

Video 5: Use of the stock types and special stock types that are newly supported in the SAP Fiori apps 'Manage Stock', 'Transfer Stock - In-Plant', and 'Transfer Stock - Cross-Plant'

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

 

Warehousing Management

Check out the blog post Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Release – What’s New in which Christian Ries shares the innovations for Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release.

In this blog post I highlight 3 innovations from Warehouse Management that my colleague Markus Oertelt demonstrated in our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series, and share the demo videos with you.

Warehouse-Driven Split of the Outbound Delivery Order

Now you can split packed and unpacked items, quantities, or handling units (HUs) from an existing outbound delivery order into a new outbound delivery order.
Use this feature to remove parts of an outbound delivery order that, for example, are not ready to be shipped or for which there is no space on the transport, by splitting those parts into a new outbound delivery order.
You can split the outbound delivery order either before or after warehouse processing has started with created or confirmed warehouse tasks. Access this feature through the Warehouse Monitor. Two new functions are available in the Warehouse Monitor for the following nodes:

  • Outbound Delivery Order --> Outbound Delivery Order Item --> More Methods --> Split Open Qty into new ODO
  • Documents --> Handling Unit --> More methods --> Split into new ODO

Watch the next video in which Markus Oertelt shows the warehouse-driven split of the outbound delivery order through two different use cases in the system:

  1. Use case - Complete picking is not possible
  2. Use case - Lack of space in the truck

Video 6: Warehouse-Driven Split of the Outbound Delivery Order

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

Generation of Packaging Items

For packed outbound deliveries packing material items can be generated automatically. The packing materials are posted goods issue in Warehouse Management (WM) and in Inventory Management (MM-IM). Generated items can be billing relevant. Items are generated with goods issue or with ready for shipping.

In the next video Markus Oertelt shows the new feature in the system and how to set it up in configuration. 

Video 7: Generation of Packaging Items

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

Partial Goods Receipt Posting

Now, you can post goods receipt for one or several handling units (HUs) that belong to the same delivery. You can post goods receipt for parts of the inbound delivery that are packed into HUs (Partial Goods Receipt). 
You can access this feature through the Warehouse Monitor. Two new functions are available on the level of the Handling Unit node:

  • Goods Receipt
    This function posts goods receipt for one or more HUs.
  • Reverse Goods Receipt
    This function reverses goods receipt for one or more HUs.

Watch the next video in which Markus Oertelt shows the new feature in the system.

Video 8: Partial Goods Receipt Posting

This feature is also available when you use radio frequency (RF) devices to carry out warehouse activities.

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

 

Transportation Management

Exciting product updates coming your way for Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408. I want to encourage you to read the latest blog post from Markus Oertelt 

  • Transportation Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 

that includes an amazing system demo about Supplier Return with Transportation Management and many more interesting topics.

(🔙 Table of Contents) 

 

More Information / Additional links

You will find a collection of SAP S/4HANA Cloud product centric blog posts from Markus Oertelt and me here:

  •  SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Manufacturing & Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management – The Link Collection
  • Discrete Industries in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - The Link Collection

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and @GerhardWelker.

 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series! 

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a xx-minute-long recording and a presentation for LoB/Industry/Topic as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below: 

 

️Thanks for reading this blog post. Stay tuned!

(🔙 Table of Contents)

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello,

I have registered for the digital skills initiative, completed the learning journey and emailed its screenshot.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: What SPs in BO 4.3 still support UNV?
entry.content: All versions of BI4.3 will support the UNV universe. Support for the UNV universes will not be supported beginning with the 2025 version of Business Objects.

entry.title: SAC - Set StoryFilter via Table
entry.content:
Hello,

is it possible to set the StoryFilter via Table cell selection?

Expected result:

Actual result:

I used the following script for the on select event for the table:

Application.getFileDataSource("").setDimensionFilterWithHierarchy("ID_FIRMA",Alias.FlatHierarchy,this.getSelections()[0]["ID_FIRMA"]);

 

The problem here is that the story filter is set to one element according to the selection. All other filter values are not visible anymore.

 

Kr

Ben

entry.title: Re: Setup of the SAP Signavio Process Insights connector within SAP Signavio Process Intelligence
entry.content: Hello Lukas, what about the Service root URL when the customer tenant is on EU10 region ? There is a different service url to call?

entry.title: Re: Introducing Identity and Authentication Management in SAP Business One Cloud
entry.content:
Hello @Guy_Sujetzki ,

Thank you for the quick response.

Customer IDPs was the thing I was looking for. I added one for the Customer: Customer Management -> Customer Details -> Identity Providers -> Add

I didn't see it in any documentation or mentioned anywhere. Also in the video on this post the IDP was also created at System Configuration -> Identity Providers

Kind Regards,

Matthias

entry.title: Re: cl_attachment_service_api usage in S/4HANA Cloud Public - Guidance Needed
entry.content: Have you checked the long text documentation of the class in ADT? It’s pretty extensive.

entry.title: Message logs not generating in COHV
entry.content:
When trying to close production orders in COHV, the logs are not generating to tell me what issues are preventing them from closing.  The function has been working perfectly for the last 18 months or so, but just stopped 3 weeks ago.

I highlight the TECO'd orders I wish to close, go to Mass Processing, Settings, Function = Close, Execute Function Immediately, Execute Changes to Data, Save Log Always and execute.

I get a message at the bottom of the screen stating Mass Processing Executed - 0 logs created.
If I attempt to close in CO02, the error message does appear.

Logs do generate for other options, such as when setting the user status or TECO'ing the orders, but they will not generate for closing.

Has anyone seen this issue before or have any ideas that I can try?  I have already cleared the cache and confirmed my access rights.

Many thanks

entry.title: Modify the Disclosure Management Landing Page Logo
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Is it possible to modify the landing page/main DM web page logo to display the company logo rather than SAP?

Thanks in advance.

Regards

Eyal

entry.title: Reading multipart form-data in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a scenario where sender sends form-data as below to Cloud Integration.

However in Cloud Integration we only need to read the main content of the file(SampleCSV.txt)  i.e. "1","2","3,a","4","5" and pass the same to target. Is there a standard way to achieve the same? Please advise

 

----------------------------705490356377119745888137
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="File"; filename="SampleCSV.txt"
Content-Type: text/plain

"1","2","3,a","4","5"
----------------------------705490356377119745888137--

 

Best Regards

entry.title: CDS VIEW ENTITY - CURR / QUAN calculated field gives activation error
entry.content:
Deal community members,

I need to create a CDS view entity with a calculated filed as CURR / QUAN to get the per unit amount.

For Example: CDS VIEW ENTITY on BSEG which should have ( DMBTR(type CURR) / MENGE(type QUAN) ) AS UNIT_PRICE

Sounds like a very simple requirement but I am facing multiple issue with this.

To start with, I get below error message:

then I tried with casting CURR field as DEC(23.2), but then I got below error :

it was surprising to see that it prompted to user UOM reference, instead of Currency reference, but nevertheless, I tried with the UoM reference, but then again a different error :

then I tried to CAST the MEINS as abap.char(3) to use it as reference for calculated field, but then a different error this time, and this is really annoying this it didn't allow casting a text value into CHAR.

I also tried to google this error message and found some conversion function but then I get different errors:

1. Using function : GET_NUMERIC_VALUE

2. using function : CURR_TO_DECFLOAT_AMOUNT 

I tried few more ways but none of them is working for me and I really wonder how can this be so challanging to achieve AMOUNT / QUANTITY in CDS VIEW ENTITY, which is a very basic arithmetic operation.

 

Same arithmetic calculation works perfectly well in ABAP code (as we have done that for decades), so now I am thinking to use Virtual elememt in CDS, but I want to achieve with the CDS approach first, without making use of ABAP layer.

 

Any tips to solve this ?

 

regards,

Bharat Bajaj

entry.title: Re: Knowledge Exchange B1 Production Module
entry.content:
Hi @LJT ,

SAP B1 Production Module Detailed,

Why the production module of SAP Business One is useful and who can leverage it. If your company does light assembly or sells items in bundles, this tool can dramatically simplify your daily routine. First of all, you need to treat a bill of material specially.

SAP Business One provides the ability to specify y a top-level item. Some SAP users refer to it as a finished material or parent. Next, you have to decide what components are child items. After that, take the production order and bring everything together, producing the corresponding parent product from its components. That’s it! 

However, this process is way more detailed, so we need to shed light upon several important nuances, such as types of bills of materials used in SAP Business One.

Bill of Material Types 

SAP Business One offers the following four BOM types: 

  • Sales. It is a sales document bill of material with a strict requirement to follow: its parent item must be a sales item. Let’s assume that you sell a gift package. It is a bundle item that contains multiple products. SAP Business One provides the ability to set it up as a sales bill of materials. The package (bundle) gets the parent item name. As for the child items, they are the inventory items inside the package. How does SAP Business One help to manage such products?
    Once the parent item is specified in a sales document, the corresponding child items appear as sub-items. Although you get the ability to update information about parent and child items, such as quantity, it is impossible to remove child items or add new sub-items to the package right in the sales document.
    Another important aspect is related to the visual representation of your sales documents. SAP Business One enables you to hide BOM components for printing when you set up the BOM. Consequently, only the parent item is represented when you print the document.
    As for the gross profit for a sales BOM, SAP Business One calculates it for the child items. 
  • Assembly. This BOM is almost identical to the one we’ve just described. The only difference is that its child items are not represented in the sales document. You can view only parent items there. However, when such documents appear in the system, SAP Business One updates the inventory level of the child items automatically.
    It is also worth mentioning that the system takes the base price for the gross profit calculation from the sum of the child items’ base prices. You can view it in the parent item’s row. 
  • Template. If you need a BOM with no restrictions, consider using this one. It supports any item type for both parent and child items. You can delete, add, or duplicate rows and make any modifications. Consequently, it is possible to use BOM templates in sales and purchasing documents for a wider variety of purposes. 
  • Production. Use this BOM type in the production order document where a parent item is a finished good while child items are its components. SAP even lets you add labor as a component as a non-inventory item that has a standard cost. 

 

In addition to the features described above, SAP Business One provides the ability to manage BOMs in bulk. Go to the Production main menu and you will discover the following two instruments:

  • Update Parent Prices Globally. This instrument provides the ability to update parent item’s price following new prices for its child items. SAP Business One analyses price lists to identify components with new prices. Next, it recalculates a new price for the parent component. 
  • Bill of Materials – Component Management. With the aid of this instrument, you can initiate transactions that change, delete, or add BOM line items, updating the existing BOMs. Let’s assume that you no longer add a specific item to several bundle products. SAP lets you remove it from all of them in bulk.

You can apply additional configurations to sales BOM prices. SAP Business One provides the ability to choose a basis for their calculation under Administration -> System Initialization -> Document Settings -> General tab. Here you can specify whether to configure the sales prices from the parent item to its children. 

Resources 

Resources represent a special type of master data utilized in the production processes. Under this term, we consider such resources used in the production as labor hours, machine capacity, other means, etc.

You should manage SAP Business One resources as follows: 

  • Create the resource master data;
  • Link it to the BOM or production order via the Resource row type;
  • Define the resource master data: its key piece of information is the capacity that displays the availability of resources by date (e.g., hours of labor available on a certain day).

The resource master data window displays the resource number, description, type, group, measure units, etc. Other information is split between the following tabs:

  • General – this tab contains information about the resource issue method, allocation, linked item, and cost;
  • Capacity Data – this tab displays capacity data per warehouse for a particular period;
  • Planning Data – this tab shows daily capacity factors;
  • Employees – this tab provides information about employees associated with this resource;
  • Properties – this tab contains resource properties;
  • Attachments – this tab stores attachments associated with the resource;
  • Remarks – this tab offers all remarks related to the resource. 

 

The resource master record displays the resource availability per the warehouse. The General tab of the resource master data lets you add up to 10 costs that compose the total resource cost. This parameter is used for calculating and posting component costs. Note that it is necessary to specify primary accounts for resource expenses and WIP in the G/L account determination to enable the correct functionality for these processes. 

You can select the Internal Resource Capacity option from the context menu to update the daily capacity of the defined period. Proceed to the Planning Data tab of the resource master data record. Here, SAP Business One provides the ability to specify the estimated daily capacity for each day of the week. For instance, your daily capacity for 3 employees that work 8 hours a day is 24 hours.

Production Order 

SAP Business One utilizes the production order documents to record the finished materials to be produced along with their components and issue methods. The system offers two ways of creating POs. Firstly, you can run through all the corresponding processes manually. Secondly, the system lets you leverage the Procurement Confirmation Wizard to automate this process.

By default, the following three types of POs are at your service:

  • Standard. With this type, components are issued while the corresponding parent is received. It is based on a production BOM. 
  • Special. Components are also issued while the corresponding parent is received. The parent item can be any inventory item while components are specified individually.
  • Disassembly. This type works in the opposite direction: the parent item is issued while child items are received. It is based on a production BOM as well.

If you need to add a production order in SAP Business One, follow these steps:

  1. Choose the production order type;
  2. Specify the parent item. 
  3. Set the parent item quantity. 
  4. Specify the production order due date (the production completion date). 
  5. Assign a sales order (optionally). 
  6. Depending on your BOM setup, components appear automatically. Add/delete them.
  7. Configure other parameters, such as the base quantities, warehouse, issue method, etc. 
  8. Now, you can add the production order. 

 

Don’t forget to change the status of your PO to Released when the production process begins. From the context menu, and choose the Issue Components option to add manual components. Report the completion of production at the end of the process to receive the finished item into stock. Also, this action triggers the cost of producing the item.

 

Also, note that items set to backflush are issued automatically. You only need to choose Report Completion. SAP Business One creates an Issue for Production document automatically. However, it is impossible to backflush items that are managed with serial numbers or batch numbers. They are bound to a manual issue method so that you have to identify the serial numbers or batches to release them from inventory. 

The Summary tab displays the components’ total cost, finished goods cost, and whether any variances between them occur for the standard costing method. Changing the production order status to Closed triggers the variance posting to the WIP variance account. Note that for Moving Average, FIFO, or Serial/Batch valuation methods, SAP Business One updates the parent cost on the basis of the component’s total cost.

entry.title: Re: Include BLANK OPTION for an attribute dropdownlist in...
entry.content:
Hello Colleagues,

If you use Value Table (+ Text Table) for your attribute and this table has an empty key, it should work out. 

So solution is --> use Value Table for the values of your Char- attribute. 

Ref: COMC_ATTRIBUTE-ATTR_TABNAME

BR, Dima

entry.title: Re: How can I Improve performance of Below code.
entry.content: Make sure you are using sorted/hashed tables with appropriate keys or BINARY SEARCH.

entry.title: Re: automation not getting executed despite unattended mode of bot and environment assignment to bot
entry.content: Facing similar issue, got any solution ?

entry.title: How to use dimension or measure as a filter in second story?
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm trying to join two stories but I want to use record from the tables from first story as a filter for second story.
In the second story I have prompts on the start and I'm wondering if I can put my chosen record as a value in the prompts?

As below, I put the hyperlink to External URL(second story), but when I go there, that what I see is dependence of what I choose in prompts. The filter is not working.


Is there any option to apply that as a one of the filter in prompts? Maybe some script?

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: Add custom field to Inbound Delivery - SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello!

Any response/guidance would be highly appreciated.

 

Regards

Snigdhodeep

entry.title: Re: Data description import from different file
entry.content: This worked, thank you! If i may ask another question, if i want to have a column with the code description instead of a description associated to the same column. Is this possible?

entry.title: Re: how to see quantity per warehouse in list of items
entry.content:
Hi @MarkOenema ,

"You can enable the 'Qty In Warehouse' column from the form settings."

If you need it to be more specific or detailed, just let me know!

entry.title: API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV returns no header pricing data
entry.content:
We are seeing no header pricing data coming back from this service and wonder if it is a bug that SAP should investigate or fix.

Even in the tryout feature, no billing documents return header pricing.

https://api.sap.com/api/API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV/tryout

Has anyone been able to retrieve header pricing conditions for billing documents with this service?

entry.title: How To hide the "Work Permit" portlet in both Employee Central (EC)for USA Employes
entry.content:
Hello, 

Can anybody  help me To find how to hide the "Work Permit" portlet in  Employee Central (EC) for USA employee only please

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Welcome @SapScm  &  Welcome @JoranDV 

When you are keen to learn more about SAP Technology, I would like to recommend upcoming Devtoberfest and SAP TechEd. Please consider joining the SAP TechEd Group or the Devtoberfest Group to get more information!

If you are wondering what sessions to attend you can also check out the Not sure which sessions to attend? We asked some S... - SAP Community.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM API & Integration: Several extensions of SAP Cloud ALM with SAP BTP (Part 3): Use
entry.content:
Hi Boris,

This isn't related to your user as the BTP destination should use the clientid/secret.

Are the scopes defined like described here https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/apis/managing-scopes?locale=en-us ?

Regards,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano 

I am a woman 

, my name is Sara and I'm excited to be part of the SAP Community Beginner Corner! I work for an organization called Edraky, which is focused on providing comprehensive SAP training and implementation services to businesses across the Middle East. I'm writing to apply for certification C_TS4CO_2021.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Function Module 'CHVW_EXPLODE_ALL '
entry.content:
I analyzed BMBC and observed the following:

  1. Type C - Displays Inspection Lot and Process Order details of the row material-batch
  2. Type A - Displays Process Order detail for the row material-batch (i.e. Header Material of corresponding Process Order)
  3. Type B - Displays Purchase Order, Delivery details for the row material-batch (i.e. Component Material of corresponding Process Order)
